title: linaria minor subsp. praetermissa
intro: Linaria minor subsp. praetermissa, also known as Toadflax or Small Toadflax, is a perennial plant species belonging to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems, due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as relieving coughs, soothing sore throats, and calming digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Linaria minor subsp. praetermissa can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
